rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HAWTHORNE PLACE OWNERS ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Hearing On Verified Petition To Reduce Required Voting Percentage To Amend Declaration Of Conditions, Covenants And Restrictions Of Hawthorne Place, A Condominium Project
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 19, 2022 line 1. PETITIONER HAWTHORNE PLACE OWNERS ASSOCIATION's Amended Motion For Hearing On Verified Petition To Reduce Required Voting Percentage To Amend Declaration Of Conditions, Covenants And Restrictions Of Hawthorne Place, A Condominium Project OFF CALENDAR no valid proof of service on file. CCP 1013a, 1013b and CRC 3.1300(c). = (501/HJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROSS B. MAKASIAN VS. ROY & IRENE SKOVER FAMILY TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Appointing Successors-In-Interest To, And Substituting Successors-In­Interest For Deceased Plaintiff, And Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint Pursuant To Stipulation And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 19, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF ROSS MAKASIAN's Motion For An Order Appointing Successors-In-Interest To, And Substituting Successors-In-Interest For Deceased Plaintiff, And Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint Pursuant To Stipulation And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities OFF CALENDAR, no valid proof of service on file. Proof of service dated July 26, 2022 appears to indicate that Plaintiff's motion was electronically served by an individual employed by Defendant's counsel on Plaintiff. No e-mail address of the person making the service is listed. CCP 1013b(b)(1). = (501/HJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VERPRESAR LLC, ET AL VS. LIN DEE LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Enjoin Wrongful Trustees Sale Until Judicial Decree
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 19, 2022 line 4. PLAINTIFFs VERPRESAR LLC and CAMIERDA I, LLC's Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Enjoin Wrongful Trustees Sale Until Judicial Decree DENIED. The following is stricken: (1) pages 12-18 of the 18-page Motion for Preliminary Injunction (no Table of Contents or Table of Authorities) and (2) unauthorized supplemental reply filed on August 16, 2022. Pursuant to the Notice of Motion a Preliminary Injunction is sought per Civil Code 2924.12, 2924.17 and 2924.19. No cases are cited for the proposition that these code sections are applicable under the circumstances of this case; no cases are cited to support Plaintiff's position that any asserted wrongdoings by the defendants constitute a violation of CC 2924.17; and no evidence is provided that any purported violation was material.= (501/HJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
